meaning: n.顿挫,挫折,退步,逆流,(疾病的)复发
examples: It is an unexpected setback in her fortunes.
A further reduction in local supplies is yet another unwelcome setback to the industry.
The move represents a setback for the Middle East peace process.
Shares in Boeing have been incredibly resilient despite the major setback suffered by its marquee aircraft.
Regardless, the decision will be seen as a setback for the DWP's flagship back-to-work schemes.
